title: toxicodendron retinorrhoeum
intro: Toxicodendron retinorrhoeum is a type of flowering plant known as the poison sumac, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, native to the southeastern United States and parts of Asia. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic resin that can cause severe skin irritation, but it also has medicinal properties and health benefits when properly prepared. The resin has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron retinorrhoeum include extracts and tinctures that can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote skin health.
